source.title: A Neural Computational Model of Incentive Salience

confidence_rationale: This is the formal computational model of incentive salience that directly formalises what MECH-216 proposes. The wanting = kappa x learned_value equation is the mathematical core. High source quality (peer-reviewed computational neuroscience with empirical validation against ventral pallidum recordings). Very high mapping fidelity -- the model's structure (cached learned prediction x current state = dynamic wanting) maps almost exactly onto E1 schema activation x drive_level = VALENCE_WANTING seeding.
failure_signatures: The model assumes a separate kappa (state) parameter that modulates wanting independently of learning -- REE does not yet have a clean kappa analog beyond drive_level.
The model's learned values are TD-cached reward predictions, not schema activations per se -- the mapping from TD values to E1 schema readout is not exact.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Incentive salience is computed as W_m(s) = kappa * f(V_hat(s), r_t), where kappa is current neurobiological state, V_hat is the TD-learned cached value, and r_t is the current reward. The model predicts ventral pallidum firing patterns during CS-triggered wanting.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-216 proposes that E1 schema activations (analog of V_hat -- learned predictive representations) seed VALENCE_WANTING (analog of W_m -- the wanting signal) at the current z_world position, modulated by drive_level (analog of kappa). The seeding mechanism is the E1-to-serotonin pathway rather than the mesolimbic dopamine pathway.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Zhang et al.'s V_hat is a scalar TD value; E1 schema activation is a high-dimensional pattern. The mapping assumes that a scalar readout (schema activation magnitude) can serve the same role as V_hat. The kappa modulation in REE is via drive_level (SD-012), which is not yet fully functional.
mapping.source_context: Computational model validated against rat ventral pallidum neural recordings during Pavlovian conditioned approach.

confidence_components.notes: Peer-reviewed computational model with neural validation. The computational structure maps very well onto MECH-216. Transfer risk low because the model is itself an abstraction of the biological mechanism -- we are mapping abstraction to abstraction.
